zh.lifeSummary: 當Sewell de Eatington於1058年出生在英國瓦維其可爾艾廷頓時，他的父親Fulcher de Eatington38歲，母親Eatington35歲。他於1085年之前與Mrs Sewell Eatington結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1129年在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃享年71歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Eatington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Edington
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the three places called Edington in Northumberland, Somerset, and Wiltshire, or from Eddington, Berkshire. Edington in Northumberland arises from Old English Idingtūn, from the Old English personal name Ida, + ing, denoting association, + tūn ‘settlement’ (compare Ida ). In Somerset, the name comes either from the Old English male name Ēadwine or from the feminine Ēadwynn + ing + tūn. Edington (Wiltshire) is from either the Old English personal name Ētha + dūn ‘hill’, or from Old English ēthe ‘uncultivated’ + dūn.

nameMeanings.name: Sewell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Siwal(d), Sewal(d), Old English Sigeweald, from sige ‘victory’ + weald ‘rule(r)’, or the corresponding Old Norse Sigvaldr. This name later became thoroughly confused with Sewal(d), from Old English Sǣweald (see Sewall ).

habitational name from one or more places, such as Sewell (Bedfordshire), Seawell (Northamptonshire), Sywell (Northamptonshire), Showell in Steeple Barton (Oxfordshire), Showell in Little Tew (Oxfordshire), or Sowell in Kentisbeare (Devon), all meaning ‘seven springs’ (Old English seofon + wella). Compare Seawell .

variants.name: Seawell
meanings: English: variant of   Sewell   and   Sewall  .

variants.name: Sewall
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Sewal(d), Sawal(d), Old English Sǣweald, from sǣ ‘sea’ + weald ‘rule(r)’. This name later became thoroughly confused with Sewal(d) from Old English Sigeweald or Old Norse Sigvaldr (see Sewell ). Compare Seawell .

variants.name: Showell
meanings: Anglicized form of the Welsh name Hywel , or a transferred use of the surname derived from that name.

variants.name: Shewell
meanings: English: variant of Sewell with palatalization of the initial S-. The pronunciation may have been influenced through folk etymology by the English dialect word shewel, for a scarecrow.

variants.name: Suell
meanings: Generally, this is a shortened form of   Michelle  , respelled by association with the vocabulary word. In some cases it may be a shortened form of   Shelley .

variants.name: Sowell
meanings: Mainly U.S.: transferred use of the surname of a well-known New England family, whose members included the poet  Robert Lowell  (  1917–77 ). The surname is a variant of   Lovell .

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Sewell de Eatington was born in 1058, in Ettington, Warwickshire, England, his father, Fulcher de Eatington, was 38 and his mother, Eatington, was 35. He married Mrs Sewell Eatington before 1085. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1129, in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sewell d’Eatington est né en 1058, à Ettington, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Fulcher d’Eatington, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Eatington, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Mrs Sewell Eatington avant 1085. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1129, à Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 71 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sewell de Eatington(이)가 1058년 영국 워윅실르 틴그턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fulcher de Eatington(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Eatington(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1085년 이전 Mrs Sewell Eatington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1129년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.
